data.title: RED: Write failing tests for preconditions-validator.sh (validator accepts, rejects, tolerates unknown fields)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## TDD Requirement
Write tests that fail before preconditions-validator.sh exists. Three behavioral scenarios:
1. Validator accepts a well-formed minimal PRECONDITIONS event (exit 0)
2. Validator rejects a schema-invalid event (exit 1 + diagnostic to stderr)
3. Validator tolerates unknown fields in the event (exit 0, depth-agnostic contract)

These tests must fail (RED) before T2 creates preconditions-validator.sh.

## Test Approach (Given / When / Then)
Test 1 (accepts well-formed):
  Given: A fixture JSON file at /tmp/fixture-preconditions-valid.json containing minimal-tier fields (event_type=PRECONDITIONS, gate_name, session_id, worktree_id, tier=minimal, timestamp, data={})
  When: bash plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-validator.sh <ticket_id> <stage> using the fixture
  Then: Exit code 0 (valid)

Test 2 (rejects schema-invalid):
  Given: A fixture JSON file missing required field gate_name
  When: bash plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-validator.sh <ticket_id> <stage> using the fixture
  Then: Exit code 1 + stderr contains diagnostic naming the missing field

Test 3 (tolerates unknown fields — depth-agnostic):
  Given: A fixture JSON file with all minimal-tier fields PLUS unknown fields decisions_log, test_contract, foo_bar
  When: bash plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-validator.sh <ticket_id> <stage>
  Then: Exit code 0 (unknown fields are ignored, not rejected)

## Implementation Notes
- Test file: tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh
- Fixtures: inline here-docs in the test file (no separate fixture dir needed)
- Validator reads from stdin OR a path argument (design detail: T2 decides interface)
  Use the same interface T2 will implement — validate by reading event files from the per-ticket event dir,
  so the test stubs _read_latest_preconditions or passes a direct file path flag.
  Preferred interface: preconditions-validator.sh <ticket_id> <stage> [--event-file=<path>]
  so tests can pass a fixture path without requiring a live tickets-tracker worktree.
- Add .test-index RED marker before committing

## File Impact
- CREATE: tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh
- MODIFY: .test-index (add RED marker for preconditions-validator.sh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh
- [ ] Test file contains test_validator_accepts_well_formed, test_validator_rejects_invalid, test_validator_tolerates_unknown_fields
  Verify: grep -c 'test_validator_accepts_well_formed\|test_validator_rejects_invalid\|test_validator_tolerates_unknown_fields' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] All 3 tests FAIL before T2 is implemented (script does not exist yet)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-preconditions-validator.sh 2>&1; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] Tests are behavioral: each test invokes preconditions-validator.sh with a fixture and asserts on exit code or stderr output (no grep-of-source-file patterns)
  Verify: manual review — test_approach in task description describes execution + assertion
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker [test_validator_accepts_well_formed] for plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-validator.sh
  Verify: grep 'preconditions-validator.sh.*\[test_validator_accepts_well_formed\]\|test_validator_accepts_well_formed.*preconditions-validator' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) after staging only this task
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately
